content:
# 单篇讲义生成任务

项目仓库: lnis-uofu/OpenFPGA
项目名: OpenFPGA
讲义目录: OpenFPGA-tutorial/
代码永久链接 base: https://github.com/lnis-uofu/OpenFPGA/blob/a1e51333d2dd85cc4a640b6e384f0c9f5d97aa4a/
当前 HEAD: a1e51333d2dd85cc4a640b6e384f0c9f5d97aa4a
动作: new


---

## 项目学习路线（大纲方法论）

# 项目学习手册·大纲规划 Planner

## 角色

你是资深技术教育专家、源码阅读导师和项目学习路线设计者。

你运行在被分析项目的根目录内。

你可以使用：

- `Read`
- `Grep`
- `Glob`
- 只读 `git` 命令，例如 `git log`、`git diff`、`git show`、`git ls-files`

你不能：

- 修改源码
- 写入任何 `.md` 文件
- 输出 JSON 以外的文字

你的唯一产出是一个学习手册大纲 manifest JSON。

---

## 总目标

为当前项目规划一套「从零开始、由浅入深、结合源码和代码实践」的学习手册。

这套手册会被拆分成多篇 Markdown 讲义，后续 worker 会根据 manifest 逐篇生成。

每篇讲义应该满足：

1. 面向初学者也能读懂。
2. 只讲一个相对独立的主题。
3. 必须结合真实源码。
4. 必须包含一个可操作的代码实践任务。
5. 讲义之间有清晰依赖关系。

---

## 源码探查顺序

请按以下顺序理解项目，不要一开始就深入细节：

1. 项目说明
   - README
   - docs
   - examples
   - tutorials

2. 构建与运行方式
   - package.json
   - Cargo.toml
   - pyproject.toml
   - go.mod
   - pom.xml
   - Makefile
   - Dockerfile
   - CI 配置

3. 项目入口
   - CLI 入口
   - Web 服务入口
   - main 文件
   - library export 文件
   - examples 入口

4. 目录结构
   - src
   - lib
   - app
   - packages
   - crates
   - tests

5. 核心流程
   - 初始化流程
   - 配置加载
   - 请求/任务/数据处理主链路
   - 插件、扩展、调度、存储等核心机制

6. 测试与示例
   - 单元测试
   - 集成测试
   - benchmark
   - example usage

---

## 学习路线设计原则

请把学习手册设计成三层：

1. 入门层 beginner
   目标：让读者知道项目是什么、怎么运行、目录如何组织、核心入口在哪里。

2. 进阶层 intermediate
   目标：让读者理解主要模块、核心调用链、关键数据结构和配置机制。

3. 专家层 advanced
   目标：让读者理解扩展点、性能、并发、错误处理、测试、二次开发和架构取舍。

---

## 讲义拆分规则

- 每篇讲义只解决一个主要问题。
- 每篇讲义包含 2 到 5 个最小模块。
- 每篇讲义必须有一个 `practice_task`，描述读者要完成的代码实践。
- 每篇讲义的源码范围要适中，通常引用 1 到 6 个关键源码文件。
- 大型项目可以拆成 30 篇以上讲义；中型项目 15 到 30 篇；小型项目 8 到 15 篇。
- 不要为了数量硬拆，逻辑优先。
- 第一单元必须帮助读者从零开始，包括项目定位、运行方式、目录结构、入口文件。
- 后续单元再进入核心模块、源码机制、扩展实践。

---

## mode=full

首次生成大纲时：

1. 阅读项目关键文件。
2. 理解项目定位、技术栈、运行方式和核心模块。
3. 设计从零开始的学习路径。
4. 输出完整 manifest。
5. 所有讲义的 `action` 都必须是 `"new"`。

---

## mode=incremental

增量更新时：

1. 读取 stdin 中提供的现有 manifest。
2. 阅读项目中已有的 `<项目名>-tutorial/` 讲义目录，理解旧结构。
3. 使用：

   - `git diff previous_head..current_head`
   - `git log`
   - `git show`

   分析代码变化。

4. 输出更新后的完整 manifest。

每篇讲义的 `action` 按以下规则设置：

- `"keep"`
  代码变化不影响这篇讲义，无需重写。

- `"update"`
  小范围改动，例如函数重命名、参数调整、局部逻辑变化、链接行号变化。

- `"new"`
  新增功能、新增模块、新增重要机制，需要新增讲义。

- `"rebuild"`
  架构、主流程、公共接口、核心模型发生重大变化，旧讲义需要重建。

如果某篇旧讲义已经不再适合当前项目结构，可以从 manifest 中移除。

---

## manifest 输出格式

只输出严格 JSON，不要输出 Markdown，不要输出解释文字。

```json
{
  "project": "<项目名>",
  "head": "<current_head>",
  "rationale": "中文说明：为什么这样设计学习顺序、单元划分和讲义顺序。",
  "units": [
    {
      "id": "u1",
      "title": "单元标题",
      "level": "beginner",
      "order": 1,
      "lectures": [
        {
          "id": "u1-l1",
          "title": "讲义标题",
          "order": 1,
          "filename": "u1-l1-project-overview.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "本讲义要讲什么，用 1 到 3 句中文说明。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "学习目标 1",
            "学习目标 2"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "README.md",
            "src/main.ts"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "模块 1",
            "模块 2"
          ],
          "practice_task": "本讲义对应的代码实践任务。",
          "depends_on": [],
          "action": "new"
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

---

## 字段要求

- `project`：项目名。
- `head`：当前 git HEAD。
- `rationale`：中文，一段即可。
- `unit.id`：格式为 `u1`、`u2`、`u3`。
- `lecture.id`：格式为 `u1-l1`、`u1-l2`。
- `filename`：
  - 必须全局唯一。
  - 必须小写。
  - 使用连字符。
  - 必须以 `.md` 结尾。
  - 文件位于 `<项目名>-tutorial/` 目录下。
- `level`：
  - `"beginner"`
  - `"intermediate"`
  - `"advanced"`
- `source_files`：
  - 只能填写你实际读过或确认存在的源码文件。
  - 不要编造文件路径。
- `depends_on`：
  - 使用讲义 id。
  - 没有依赖时填写空数组。
- `action`：
  - full 模式全部为 `"new"`。
  - incremental 模式使用 `"keep"`、`"update"`、`"new"`、`"rebuild"`。

---

## 重要约束

- 只基于真实源码和项目文件规划。
- 不确定的地方写 `"待确认"`。
- 不要编造不存在的模块、命令、接口或源码文件。
- 输出必须是合法 JSON。


## 完整大纲

```json
{
  "project": "OpenFPGA",
  "rationale": "OpenFPGA 是一个用 C++ 编写、经过流片验证的开源 FPGA IP 生成框架，代码量大（仅 openfpga/src 下就有 200+ 个源文件，外加 13 个独立支撑库）。因此学习手册按「先会用、再懂原理、最后能扩展」的三层渐进设计。\n\n入门层（u1–u3）让读者从零开始：先认识项目定位与目录结构，学会用 CMake/Makefile 编译并用 Python 脚本跑通第一个设计流；再理解 openfpga 可执行程序的 shell 入口、命令分组与 OpenfpgaContext 全局数据中枢；最后搞清楚驱动整个流程的两套输入文件（VPR 架构 XML 与 openfpga_arch.xml），重点是电路库与配置协议。这一层遵循「定位→编译→运行→理解输入」的真实上手顺序。\n\n进阶层（u4–u8）沿着 canonical example_script.openfpga 的真实命令链展开：架构加载与 VPR 标注 → build_fabric 构建 ModuleManager 模块图 → 两级比特流模型与生成 → Verilog/SPICE/SDC 网表与约束输出。每个单元对应一条主链路上的核心子系统，讲义之间存在严格的上下游依赖（例如 fabric 比特流依赖模块图与 architecture 比特流），保证读者按顺序学习时不会出现知识断层。\n\n专家层（u9–u10）面向二次开发与架构取舍：存储器组/移位寄存器 bank、时钟网络、repack、tile 直连、GSB 压缩等高级机制，以及支撑库（util/mux/decoder/pcf/bus_group/mif 等）的内部实现，最后落到如何用 Shell<T> 框架新增命令、如何用回归测试与 CI 参与贡献。讲义数量（41 篇）与项目规模匹配，所有源码引用均经过实际核对，不依赖硬拆。",
  "units": [
    {
      "id": "u1",
      "title": "认识 OpenFPGA：项目定位、编译与首次运行",
      "level": "beginner",
      "order": 1,
      "lectures": [
        {
          "id": "u1-l1",
          "title": "OpenFPGA 是什么：FPGA IP 生成与完整 EDA 流程",
          "order": 1,
          "filename": "u1-l1-project-overview.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "介绍 OpenFPGA 作为开源 FPGA IP 生成器的定位、它覆盖的 Verilog-to-Bitstream 全流程，以及它在 EDA 生态（VPR、Yosys）中的角色。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "理解 FPGA IP 生成的含义与 OpenFPGA 的核心价值",
            "说出 OpenFPGA 端到端流程的主要阶段：综合、布局布线、fabric 构建、比特流/网表生成",
            "了解 OpenFPGA 与 VPR、Yosys 等子模块的协作关系"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "README.md",
            "VERSION.md",
            "docs/source/overview/figures/OpenFPGA_logo.png"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "项目说明 README",
            "版本信息"
          ],
          "practice_task": "阅读 README 与引用论文摘要，用一段话写出 OpenFPGA 解决了什么问题、它的输入和输出分别是什么，并画出 Verilog→Bitstream 的粗粒度流程框图。",
          "depends_on": [],
          "action": "new"
        },
        {
          "id": "u1-l2",
          "title": "仓库目录结构解析",
          "order": 2,
          "filename": "u1-l2-repository-structure.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "讲解仓库顶层目录布局：openfpga/ 核心引擎、libs/ 支撑库、openfpga_flow/ 流程脚本与数据、子模块（vtr-verilog-to-routing、yosys）、docs 与 dev。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "掌握仓库顶层各目录的职责划分",
            "区分核心引擎源码（openfpga/src）与可复用库（libs/）",
            "定位流程脚本、架构文件、任务与基准测试所在位置"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "README.md",
            "CMakeLists.txt",
            "openfpga.sh"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "仓库布局",
            "openfpga/ 引擎",
            "libs/ 库",
            "openfpga_flow/ 流程"
          ],
          "practice_task": "在仓库根目录下制作一张目录树速查表，标注 openfpga/src、libs、openfpga_flow/scripts、openfpga_flow/openfpga_arch、openfpga_flow/vpr_arch、openfpga_flow/tasks 各自存放什么。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u1-l1"
          ],
          "action": "new"
        },
        {
          "id": "u1-l3",
          "title": "从源码编译与运行环境搭建",
          "order": 3,
          "filename": "u1-l3-build-and-run.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "讲解如何用 Makefile + CMake 编译 OpenFPGA，子模块初始化（checkout），构建类型与目标（make compile / cmake_goals=openfpga），以及 openfpga.sh 环境变量脚本的作用。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "掌握 make checkout 与 make compile 的完整构建流程",
            "理解 BUILD_TYPE、CMAKE_FLAGS、OPENFPGA_WITH_INSTALLER 等构建选项",
            "学会 source openfpga.sh 配置 OPENFPGA_PATH 等环境变量"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "Makefile",
            "CMakeLists.txt",
            "openfpga.sh",
            "Dockerfile"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "Makefile 构建系统",
            "CMake 配置",
            "子模块 checkout",
            "openfpga.sh 环境脚本"
          ],
          "practice_task": "执行 make checkout 初始化子模块，再用 make compile 构建 openfpga 可执行文件，确认 build 目录下生成了 openfpga 二进制，并运行 openfpga --version。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u1-l2"
          ],
          "action": "new"
        },
        {
          "id": "u1-l4",
          "title": "运行第一个 FPGA 设计流",
          "order": 4,
          "filename": "u1-l4-first-fpga-flow.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "用 openfpga_flow 提供的 Python 脚本与任务配置跑通一个最小示例（如 and2），理解 run_fpga_flow.py 单次流程与 run_fpga_task.py 批量任务的区别，以及 task.conf 的结构。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "区分 run-task / run-flow / rerun-task 等 openfpga.sh 快捷命令",
            "读懂 task.conf 的 [GENERAL]/[OpenFPGA_SHELL]/[ARCHITECTURES]/[BENCHMARKS] 等段落",
            "成功跑通一个 micro_benchmark 并定位输出目录"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "openfpga.sh",
            "openfpga_flow/scripts/run_fpga_flow.py",
            "openfpga_flow/scripts/run_fpga_task.py",
            "openfpga_flow/tasks/basic_tests/full_testbench/configuration_chain/config/task.conf"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "run_fpga_task.py 任务调度",
            "run_fpga_flow.py 单次流程",
            "task.conf 配置",
            "openfpga.sh 快捷函数"
          ],
          "practice_task": "source openfpga.sh 后运行 run-task basic_tests/full_testbench/configuration_chain，观察 run 目录产出，并用 goto-task 进入结果目录查看生成的 fabric 网表与比特流。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u1-l3"
          ],
          "action": "new"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "u2",
      "title": "OpenFPGA Shell 入门：入口、命令与全局数据",
      "level": "beginner",
      "order": 2,
      "lectures": [
        {
          "id": "u2-l1",
          "title": "openfpga 程序入口与三种运行模式",
          "order": 1,
          "filename": "u2-l1-shell-entrypoint.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "从 main.cpp 出发，跟踪 OpenfpgaShell::start() 如何解析命令行参数并进入交互模式、脚本模式或 execute 模式。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "理解 main.cpp → OpenfpgaShell::start() 的启动链路",
            "区分 interactive / script (-f) / execute (-x) / batch 四种运行模式",
            "掌握 --file、--execute、--version、--help 等启动选项"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "openfpga/src/main.cpp",
            "openfpga/src/base/openfpga_shell.cpp",
            "openfpga/src/base/openfpga_shell.h",
            "openfpga/src/base/openfpga_title.cpp"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "main 入口",
            "OpenfpgaShell::start",
            "shell 运行模式",
            "标题与版本输出"
          ],
          "practice_task": "分别用 openfpga（交互，输入 help 再 exit）、openfpga -f example_script.openfpga（脚本）、openfpga -x 'version; exit'（execute）三种方式启动，对比输出差异。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u1-l3"
          ],
          "action": "new"
        },
        {
          "id": "u2-l2",
          "title": "命令分组与注册机制",
          "order": 2,
          "filename": "u2-l2-command-registration.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "讲解 OpenFPGA 的七大命令类别（VPR、setup、verilog、bitstream、spice、sdc、basic）及其在 openfpga_shell.cpp 中通过 add_*_commands() 的注册顺序，列出最常用的用户命令。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "说出七大命令类别及其职责（如 FPGA-Verilog 负责网表生成）",
            "识别 read_openfpga_arch、link_openfpga_arch、build_fabric、repack、build_fabric_bitstream、write_fabric_verilog 等核心命令",
            "理解命令注册顺序与类别归属的来源（*_command_template.h）"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "openfpga/src/base/openfpga_shell.cpp",
            "openfpga/src/base/openfpga_setup_command_template.h",
            "openfpga/src/base/openfpga_bitstream_command_template.h",
            "openfpga/src/base/openfpga_verilog_command_template.h",
            "openfpga/src/vpr_wrapper/vpr_command.cpp"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "命令类别注册",
            "setup 命令组",
            "bitstream 命令组",
            "verilog/sdc/spice 命令组",
            "vpr 命令"
          ],
          "practice_task": "在交互式 shell 中运行 help，把列出的命令按七大类别归类整理成一张表，并为每个类别各挑一个命令用 --help 查看其选项。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u2-l1"
          ],
          "action": "new"
        },
        {
          "id": "u2-l3",
          "title": "OpenfpgaContext：贯穿全流程的全局数据中枢",
          "order": 3,
          "filename": "u2-l3-openfpga-context.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "讲解 OpenfpgaContext 作为命令间数据交换的唯一枢纽，它继承自 VPR Context，聚合了 arch、module_graph、bitstream、mux_lib 等所有核心数据，并通过 const 访问器与 mutable 访问器控制读写。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "理解 OpenfpgaContext 为何只创建一次、不可拷贝",
            "识别 context 中的关键数据成员（arch_、module_graph_、bitstream_manager_、device_rr_gsb_ 等）",
            "掌握 const 访问器（只读）与 mutable 访问器（可写）的使用约定"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "openfpga/src/base/openfpga_context.h",
            "openfpga/src/base/openfpga_flow_manager.h",
            "openfpga/src/base/openfpga_shell.h"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "OpenfpgaContext 数据聚合",
            "访问器设计模式",
            "FlowManager 流程状态"
          ],
          "practice_task": "在 openfpga_context.h 中找出与「架构、模块图、比特流、多路选择器库」对应的成员与访问器，画出命令之间通过 context 传递数据的示意图（如 read_openfpga_arch 写 arch_，build_fabric 读 arch_ 写 module_graph_）。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u2-l2"
          ],
          "action": "new"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "u3",
      "title": "架构描述与输入文件",
      "level": "beginner",
      "order": 3,
      "lectures": [
        {
          "id": "u3-l1",
          "title": "两套架构文件：VPR arch 与 openfpga_arch",
          "order": 1,
          "filename": "u3-l1-two-arch-files.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "讲清 OpenFPGA 需要两套互补的架构描述：VPR 架构 XML 描述器件结构（tileable、逻辑块、布线），openfpga_arch.xml 描述电路级物理实现（电路模型、配置协议）。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "区分 VPR 架构 XML 与 openfpga_arch.xml 的职责边界",
            "理解 task.conf 中 arch0 与 openfpga_arch_file 两个配置项分别指向哪种文件",
            "认识 openfpga_flow/vpr_arch 与 openfpga_flow/openfpga_arch 目录的内容"
          ],
```

## 前置讲义摘要（你必须基于这些已建立的认知继续，不要重复，要承接）

### u1-l1 OpenFPGA 是什么：FPGA IP 生成与完整 EDA 流程

本讲是手册开篇，旨在建立全局地图。核心结论：OpenFPGA 是首个有流片验证（silicon proofs）的开源 FPGA IP 生成器——它帮用户「造 FPGA 并给出配套 CAD 工具」，而非「在已有 FPGA 上跑设计」。它用一份 XML 架构描述同时自动生成完整 FPGA fabric 的 Verilog 网表与比特流工具，把传统一年以上的手工流程压到约一天。端到端流程分四阶段：综合（Yosys）→ 布局布线打包（VPR）→ fabric 构建（OpenFPGA）→ 网表/比特流/约束生成（OpenFPGA）。输入为用户 Verilog + VPR 架构 XML + openfpga_arch.xml；输出为 fabric Verilog 网表、配置比特流、SDC 时序约束、可选 SPICE 网表与自检 testbench。项目由 FPGA-Verilog、FPGA-SDC、FPGA-Bitstream、FPGA-SPICE 四部分组成；版本号集中于 VERSION.md（当前 1.2.4307）作单一数据源。本讲引入的关键术语：FPGA、可编程 fabric、比特流、EDA、综合、布局布线、VPR、Yosys、fabric 构建，供后续讲义承接。
### u1-l2 仓库目录结构解析

本讲解析 OpenFPGA 仓库目录结构，将顶层划分为 5 大功能区：核心引擎 `openfpga/`、可复用支撑库 `libs/`、流程脚本与数据 `openfpga_flow/`、第三方子模块（VPR、Yosys、yosys-slang）、文档与工程化。核心结论：顶层 `CMakeLists.txt` 的三条 `add_subdirectory`（vtr→libs→openfpga）是编译依赖顺序的权威证据，而 `openfpga_flow/`、`docs/` 等不参与 C++ 编译。`openfpga/src/` 按流程阶段拆为 `base/`、`fabric/`、`mux_lib/`、`repack/`、`fpga_bitstream/`、`fpga_verilog/`、`fpga_spice/`、`fpga_sdc/`、`annotation/` 等子系统目录；`libs/` 含 13 个低耦合可复用库（如 `libopenfpgautil`、`libarchopenfpga`、`libopenfpgashell`），是引擎的积木。本讲引入的关键术语：git 子模块（克隆后目录为空，须 `make checkout` 填充）、构建系统（CMake 生成 Makefile，顶层工程名为 `OpenFPGA-tool-suites`）、引擎与库的分离，以及 `openfpga_flow/` 下的流程脚本（`run_fpga_task.py`、`run_fpga_flow.py`）、回归任务（每个含 `config/task.conf`）与两套架构 XML（`vpr_arch/` 描述器件结构、`openfpga_arch/` 描述电路级物理实现，二者职责边界是 u3 的核心内容）。版本号单一数据源为 `VERSION.md`。
### u1-l3 从源码编译与运行环境搭建

本讲讲的是如何从空仓库编出 `openfpga` 二进制并配置运行环境。核心结论：OpenFPGA 是一套工具，依赖 yosys（综合）、vtr（布局布线）、yosys-slang 三个 git 子模块；clone 后这些目录为空，必须先 `make checkout`（即 `git submodule update --init --recursive`）填满，这是编译的绝对前置条件。关键概念：构建是两层结构，外层 Makefile 仅作封装、内层 CMake 才是内核。三个核心目标为 checkout（拉子模块）、compile（背后是 prebuild 的 cmake 配置加 `make -C build`）、all（前两者串行，全新环境首选）。可调参数有 BUILD_TYPE（release/debug）、cmake_goals（缩小编译范围，如只编 openfpga）、CMAKE_FLAGS（传 `-D` 给 CMake）。CMakeLists.txt 通过一组 `OPENFPGA_WITH_*` 选项（如 YOSYS、INSTALLER）控制构建范围，版本号从 VERSION.md 读取（当前 1.2.4307），子工程按 vtr→libs→openfpga 顺序纳入，要求 C++20。最后 `source openfpga.sh`（须在仓库根目录）在当前 shell 设置 OPENFPGA_PATH 等变量并定义 run-task、goto-task 等函数，为下一讲跑设计流铺路。
### u1-l4 运行第一个 FPGA 设计流

本讲是把上一讲编出的 `openfpga` 二进制真正跑起来，完成从 Verilog 到比特流的端到端流程。核心是三层调用链：`openfpga.sh` 定义的快捷函数（`run-task`/`run-flow`/`goto-task` 等）→ 批量调度器 `run_fpga_task.py` → 单次流程执行器 `run_fpga_flow.py` → `openfpga -batch -f` 执行 `.openfpga` 脚本。`openfpga.sh` 供 `source` 加载，导出 `OPENFPGA_PATH` 等环境变量与 bash 函数（必须用 source 而非 bash 执行）。一个任务即一个含 `config/task.conf` 的目录，`task.conf` 用 INI 段落（`[GENERAL]`/`[OpenFPGA_SHELL]`/`[ARCHITECTURES]`/`[BENCHMARKS]`/`[SYNTHESIS_PARAM]`）描述「架构 × 基准 × 脚本参数」，job 数为三者笛卡尔积。调度器用 `runXXX` 编号 + `latest` 软链接组织多次运行，多线程经 `Semaphore(maxthreads)` 限流，汇总 `task_result.csv`。关键结论：fabric 网表与比特流并非 Python 生成，而是由 `openfpga` 二进制执行 `.openfpga` 模板（如 `write_full_testbench_example_script.openfpga`）的命令链产出，`run_fpga_flow.py` 仅做 Yosys 综合、模板变量替换（`safe_substitute`）与编排。引入的关键术语：任务/job、`task.conf`、配置协议 `cc`（scan_chain）、运行模式 `-batch -f`，这些为 u2（shell 命令）、u3（架构文件）承接。
### u2-l1 openfpga 程序入口与三种运行模式

本讲精读 `openfpga` 入口与启动分支。`main()` 极简，仅构造 `OpenfpgaShell` 并把命令行透传给 `start()`；该类内部持有命令引擎 `shell_`（即 `Shell<OpenfpgaContext>`）与全局数据中枢 `openfpga_ctx_`。构造函数在 `start()` 之前已把七大命令组注册进 `shell_`（basic 组须最后注册）。`start()` 用 OpenFPGA 自带的 `Command` 框架定义六个选项 `-i/-f/-x/-batch/-v/-h` 并复用 `parse_command` 解析——启动本身被当作一条命令处理。分支优先级为 `version`→`interactive`→`execute`→`script`，全部不命中则打印帮助并返回 1，故裸跑 `openfpga` 不会进入交互模式，需显式 `-i`。三种 `run_*_mode` 分别对应键盘逐行、文件脚本（支持 `#` 注释与 `\` 续行）、`;` 分隔字符串；`--batch_execution` 仅是脚本模式的修饰符，决定致命错误时是立即退出还是转入交互。标题（ASCII art + 许可证）在三种模式启动时打印，版本信息（5 行元数据，源出构建生成的 `openfpga_version.h`）仅在 `--version` 打印；退出码由各命令执行状态（成功/致命/轻微）汇总，出现致命或轻微错误即为 1。
### u2-l2 命令分组与注册机制

本讲解析 OpenFPGA 的命令分组与注册机制。OpenFPGA 在 `OpenfpgaShell` 构造函数里按固定顺序注册七大命令类别：VPR、OpenFPGA setup、FPGA-Verilog、FPGA-Bitstream、FPGA-SPICE、FPGA-SDC、Basic，对应七个 `add_*_commands` 函数。顺序不可调换，因为后续组要靠 `shell.command("名字")` 反查前置命令（如 setup 反查 `vpr`、其余组反查 `build_fabric`）来构造依赖边。

引入的关键术语：**命令类别**（仅用于 `help` 分组显示的标签）、**Shell<T>**（自带命令框架，`T` 为 `OpenfpgaContext`）、**MACRO 命令**（自带解析器、shell 透传整行参数的黑盒，如 `vpr`）、**执行函数类型**（`set_command_const_execute_function` 只读 context、`set_command_execute_function` 可写 context）、**命令依赖**（运行时硬约束，非注释）。

核心结论：每条命令遵循**四步注册法**——建 `Command` 加选项 → `add_command` 取 id → `set_command_class` 归类 → 绑定执行函数并可选声明依赖。setup 组主链为 `read_openfpga_arch → link_openfpga_arch → build_fabric`；bitstream 组有教科书级依赖链 `repack → build_architecture_bitstream → build_fabric_bitstream → write_fabric_bitstream`。`shell.tpp` 的 `execute_command` 在调用执行函数前遍历依赖列表，前置命令未运行或失败即报错返回，依赖只检查一级、不递归。**Basic 组必须最后注册**，且组内 `help` 必须最后添加，因其执行函数要对已注册命令集做快照来打印帮助。
### u2-l3 OpenfpgaContext：贯穿全流程的全局数据中枢

本讲讲解 OpenfpgaContext——OpenFPGA shell 中命令间交换数据的唯一全局中枢。它由 OpenfpgaShell 唯一持有，只创建一次、不可拷贝，继承自 VPR 的 Context，并聚合架构（arch_）、Fabric 模块图（module_graph_）、两级比特流（bitstream_manager_/fabric_bitstream_）、多路选择器库（mux_lib_）等核心数据。关键设计是 const/mutable 两套访问器：arch() 只读返回 const T&，mutable_arch() 可写返回 T&，命名即意图，并与 set_command_const_execute_function（只读）/set_command_execute_function（可写）两种执行函数一一对应，使「命令是否改数据」在注册时即声明。FlowManager 记录跨命令的流程级开关（如 compress_routing），由产生副作用的命令写入、供下游查询。核心结论：命令的先后顺序本质就是 Context 各分区的读写依赖；本讲将 arch_ 视为黑盒，留待 u3 打开。

完成后，用一句话总结本讲义覆盖的最小模块。

# 单篇讲义生成任务

项目仓库: lnis-uofu/OpenFPGA
项目名: OpenFPGA
讲义目录: OpenFPGA-tutorial/
代码永久链接 base: https://github.com/lnis-uofu/OpenFPGA/blob/a1e51333d2dd85cc4a640b6e384f0c9f5d97aa4a/
当前 HEAD: a1e51333d2dd85cc4a640b6e384f0c9f5d97aa4a
动作: new


---

## 项目学习路线（大纲方法论）

# 项目学习手册·大纲规划 Planner

## 角色

你是资深技术教育专家、源码阅读导师和项目学习路线设计者。

你运行在被分析项目的根目录内。

你可以使用：

- `Read`
- `Grep`
- `Glob`
- 只读 `git` 命令，例如 `git log`、`git diff`、`git show`、`git ls-files`

你不能：

- 修改源码
- 写入任何 `.md` 文件
- 输出 JSON 以外的文字

你的唯一产出是一个学习手册大纲 manifest JSON。

---

## 总目标

为当前项目规划一套「从零开始、由浅入深、结合源码和代码实践」的学习手册。

这套手册会被拆分成多篇 Markdown 讲义，后续 worker 会根据 manifest 逐篇生成。

每篇讲义应该满足：

1. 面向初学者也能读懂。
2. 只讲一个相对独立的主题。
3. 必须结合真实源码。
4. 必须包含一个可操作的代码实践任务。
5. 讲义之间有清晰依赖关系。

---

## 源码探查顺序

请按以下顺序理解项目，不要一开始就深入细节：

1. 项目说明
   - README
   - docs
   - examples
   - tutorials

2. 构建与运行方式
   - package.json
   - Cargo.toml
   - pyproject.toml
   - go.mod
   - pom.xml
   - Makefile
   - Dockerfile
   - CI 配置

3. 项目入口
   - CLI 入口
   - Web 服务入口
   - main 文件
   - library export 文件
   - examples 入口

4. 目录结构
   - src
   - lib
   - app
   - packages
   - crates
   - tests

5. 核心流程
   - 初始化流程
   - 配置加载
   - 请求/任务/数据处理主链路
   - 插件、扩展、调度、存储等核心机制

6. 测试与示例
   - 单元测试
   - 集成测试
   - benchmark
   - example usage

---

## 学习路线设计原则

请把学习手册设计成三层：

1. 入门层 beginner
   目标：让读者知道项目是什么、怎么运行、目录如何组织、核心入口在哪里。

2. 进阶层 intermediate
   目标：让读者理解主要模块、核心调用链、关键数据结构和配置机制。

3. 专家层 advanced
   目标：让读者理解扩展点、性能、并发、错误处理、测试、二次开发和架构取舍。

---

## 讲义拆分规则

- 每篇讲义只解决一个主要问题。
- 每篇讲义包含 2 到 5 个最小模块。
- 每篇讲义必须有一个 `practice_task`，描述读者要完成的代码实践。
- 每篇讲义的源码范围要适中，通常引用 1 到 6 个关键源码文件。
- 大型项目可以拆成 30 篇以上讲义；中型项目 15 到 30 篇；小型项目 8 到 15 篇。
- 不要为了数量硬拆，逻辑优先。
- 第一单元必须帮助读者从零开始，包括项目定位、运行方式、目录结构、入口文件。
- 后续单元再进入核心模块、源码机制、扩展实践。

---

## mode=full

首次生成大纲时：

1. 阅读项目关键文件。
2. 理解项目定位、技术栈、运行方式和核心模块。
3. 设计从零开始的学习路径。
4. 输出完整 manifest。
5. 所有讲义的 `action` 都必须是 `"new"`。

---

## mode=incremental

增量更新时：

1. 读取 stdin 中提供的现有 manifest。
2. 阅读项目中已有的 `<项目名>-tutorial/` 讲义目录，理解旧结构。
3. 使用：

   - `git diff previous_head..current_head`
   - `git log`
   - `git show`

   分析代码变化。

4. 输出更新后的完整 manifest。

每篇讲义的 `action` 按以下规则设置：

- `"keep"`
  代码变化不影响这篇讲义，无需重写。

- `"update"`
  小范围改动，例如函数重命名、参数调整、局部逻辑变化、链接行号变化。

- `"new"`
  新增功能、新增模块、新增重要机制，需要新增讲义。

- `"rebuild"`
  架构、主流程、公共接口、核心模型发生重大变化，旧讲义需要重建。

如果某篇旧讲义已经不再适合当前项目结构，可以从 manifest 中移除。

---

## manifest 输出格式

只输出严格 JSON，不要输出 Markdown，不要输出解释文字。

```json
{
  "project": "<项目名>",
  "head": "<current_head>",
  "rationale": "中文说明：为什么这样设计学习顺序、单元划分和讲义顺序。",
  "units": [
    {
      "id": "u1",
      "title": "单元标题",
      "level": "beginner",
      "order": 1,
      "lectures": [
        {
          "id": "u1-l1",
          "title": "讲义标题",
          "order": 1,
          "filename": "u1-l1-project-overview.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "本讲义要讲什么，用 1 到 3 句中文说明。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "学习目标 1",
            "学习目标 2"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "README.md",
            "src/main.ts"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "模块 1",
            "模块 2"
          ],
          "practice_task": "本讲义对应的代码实践任务。",
          "depends_on": [],
          "action": "new"
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

---

## 字段要求

- `project`：项目名。
- `head`：当前 git HEAD。
- `rationale`：中文，一段即可。
- `unit.id`：格式为 `u1`、`u2`、`u3`。
- `lecture.id`：格式为 `u1-l1`、`u1-l2`。
- `filename`：
  - 必须全局唯一。
  - 必须小写。
  - 使用连字符。
  - 必须以 `.md` 结尾。
  - 文件位于 `<项目名>-tutorial/` 目录下。
- `level`：
  - `"beginner"`
  - `"intermediate"`
  - `"advanced"`
- `source_files`：
  - 只能填写你实际读过或确认存在的源码文件。
  - 不要编造文件路径。
- `depends_on`：
  - 使用讲义 id。
  - 没有依赖时填写空数组。
- `action`：
  - full 模式全部为 `"new"`。
  - incremental 模式使用 `"keep"`、`"update"`、`"new"`、`"rebuild"`。

---

## 重要约束

- 只基于真实源码和项目文件规划。
- 不确定的地方写 `"待确认"`。
- 不要编造不存在的模块、命令、接口或源码文件。
- 输出必须是合法 JSON。

## 前置讲义摘要（你必须基于这些已建立的认知继续，不要重复，要承接）

### u1-l1 OpenFPGA 是什么：FPGA IP 生成与完整 EDA 流程

本讲是手册开篇，旨在建立全局地图。核心结论：OpenFPGA 是首个有流片验证（silicon proofs）的开源 FPGA IP 生成器——它帮用户「造 FPGA 并给出配套 CAD 工具」，而非「在已有 FPGA 上跑设计」。它用一份 XML 架构描述同时自动生成完整 FPGA fabric 的 Verilog 网表与比特流工具，把传统一年以上的手工流程压到约一天。端到端流程分四阶段：综合（Yosys）→ 布局布线打包（VPR）→ fabric 构建（OpenFPGA）→ 网表/比特流/约束生成（OpenFPGA）。输入为用户 Verilog + VPR 架构 XML + openfpga_arch.xml；输出为 fabric Verilog 网表、配置比特流、SDC 时序约束、可选 SPICE 网表与自检 testbench。项目由 FPGA-Verilog、FPGA-SDC、FPGA-Bitstream、FPGA-SPICE 四部分组成；版本号集中于 VERSION.md（当前 1.2.4307）作单一数据源。本讲引入的关键术语：FPGA、可编程 fabric、比特流、EDA、综合、布局布线、VPR、Yosys、fabric 构建，供后续讲义承接。
### u1-l2 仓库目录结构解析

本讲解析 OpenFPGA 仓库目录结构，将顶层划分为 5 大功能区：核心引擎 `openfpga/`、可复用支撑库 `libs/`、流程脚本与数据 `openfpga_flow/`、第三方子模块（VPR、Yosys、yosys-slang）、文档与工程化。核心结论：顶层 `CMakeLists.txt` 的三条 `add_subdirectory`（vtr→libs→openfpga）是编译依赖顺序的权威证据，而 `openfpga_flow/`、`docs/` 等不参与 C++ 编译。`openfpga/src/` 按流程阶段拆为 `base/`、`fabric/`、`mux_lib/`、`repack/`、`fpga_bitstream/`、`fpga_verilog/`、`fpga_spice/`、`fpga_sdc/`、`annotation/` 等子系统目录；`libs/` 含 13 个低耦合可复用库（如 `libopenfpgautil`、`libarchopenfpga`、`libopenfpgashell`），是引擎的积木。本讲引入的关键术语：git 子模块（克隆后目录为空，须 `make checkout` 填充）、构建系统（CMake 生成 Makefile，顶层工程名为 `OpenFPGA-tool-suites`）、引擎与库的分离，以及 `openfpga_flow/` 下的流程脚本（`run_fpga_task.py`、`run_fpga_flow.py`）、回归任务（每个含 `config/task.conf`）与两套架构 XML（`vpr_arch/` 描述器件结构、`openfpga_arch/` 描述电路级物理实现，二者职责边界是 u3 的核心内容）。版本号单一数据源为 `VERSION.md`。
### u1-l3 从源码编译与运行环境搭建

本讲讲的是如何从空仓库编出 `openfpga` 二进制并配置运行环境。核心结论：OpenFPGA 是一套工具，依赖 yosys（综合）、vtr（布局布线）、yosys-slang 三个 git 子模块；clone 后这些目录为空，必须先 `make checkout`（即 `git submodule update --init --recursive`）填满，这是编译的绝对前置条件。关键概念：构建是两层结构，外层 Makefile 仅作封装、内层 CMake 才是内核。三个核心目标为 checkout（拉子模块）、compile（背后是 prebuild 的 cmake 配置加 `make -C build`）、all（前两者串行，全新环境首选）。可调参数有 BUILD_TYPE（release/debug）、cmake_goals（缩小编译范围，如只编 openfpga）、CMAKE_FLAGS（传 `-D` 给 CMake）。CMakeLists.txt 通过一组 `OPENFPGA_WITH_*` 选项（如 YOSYS、INSTALLER）控制构建范围，版本号从 VERSION.md 读取（当前 1.2.4307），子工程按 vtr→libs→openfpga 顺序纳入，要求 C++20。最后 `source openfpga.sh`（须在仓库根目录）在当前 shell 设置 OPENFPGA_PATH 等变量并定义 run-task、goto-task 等函数，为下一讲跑设计流铺路。
### u1-l4 运行第一个 FPGA 设计流

本讲是把上一讲编出的 `openfpga` 二进制真正跑起来，完成从 Verilog 到比特流的端到端流程。核心是三层调用链：`openfpga.sh` 定义的快捷函数（`run-task`/`run-flow`/`goto-task` 等）→ 批量调度器 `run_fpga_task.py` → 单次流程执行器 `run_fpga_flow.py` → `openfpga -batch -f` 执行 `.openfpga` 脚本。`openfpga.sh` 供 `source` 加载，导出 `OPENFPGA_PATH` 等环境变量与 bash 函数（必须用 source 而非 bash 执行）。一个任务即一个含 `config/task.conf` 的目录，`task.conf` 用 INI 段落（`[GENERAL]`/`[OpenFPGA_SHELL]`/`[ARCHITECTURES]`/`[BENCHMARKS]`/`[SYNTHESIS_PARAM]`）描述「架构 × 基准 × 脚本参数」，job 数为三者笛卡尔积。调度器用 `runXXX` 编号 + `latest` 软链接组织多次运行，多线程经 `Semaphore(maxthreads)` 限流，汇总 `task_result.csv`。关键结论：fabric 网表与比特流并非 Python 生成，而是由 `openfpga` 二进制执行 `.openfpga` 模板（如 `write_full_testbench_example_script.openfpga`）的命令链产出，`run_fpga_flow.py` 仅做 Yosys 综合、模板变量替换（`safe_substitute`）与编排。引入的关键术语：任务/job、`task.conf`、配置协议 `cc`（scan_chain）、运行模式 `-batch -f`，这些为 u2（shell 命令）、u3（架构文件）承接。
### u2-l1 openfpga 程序入口与三种运行模式

本讲精读 `openfpga` 入口与启动分支。`main()` 极简，仅构造 `OpenfpgaShell` 并把命令行透传给 `start()`；该类内部持有命令引擎 `shell_`（即 `Shell<OpenfpgaContext>`）与全局数据中枢 `openfpga_ctx_`。构造函数在 `start()` 之前已把七大命令组注册进 `shell_`（basic 组须最后注册）。`start()` 用 OpenFPGA 自带的 `Command` 框架定义六个选项 `-i/-f/-x/-batch/-v/-h` 并复用 `parse_command` 解析——启动本身被当作一条命令处理。分支优先级为 `version`→`interactive`→`execute`→`script`，全部不命中则打印帮助并返回 1，故裸跑 `openfpga` 不会进入交互模式，需显式 `-i`。三种 `run_*_mode` 分别对应键盘逐行、文件脚本（支持 `#` 注释与 `\` 续行）、`;` 分隔字符串；`--batch_execution` 仅是脚本模式的修饰符，决定致命错误时是立即退出还是转入交互。标题（ASCII art + 许可证）在三种模式启动时打印，版本信息（5 行元数据，源出构建生成的 `openfpga_version.h`）仅在 `--version` 打印；退出码由各命令执行状态（成功/致命/轻微）汇总，出现致命或轻微错误即为 1。
### u2-l2 命令分组与注册机制

本讲解析 OpenFPGA 的命令分组与注册机制。OpenFPGA 在 `OpenfpgaShell` 构造函数里按固定顺序注册七大命令类别：VPR、OpenFPGA setup、FPGA-Verilog、FPGA-Bitstream、FPGA-SPICE、FPGA-SDC、Basic，对应七个 `add_*_commands` 函数。顺序不可调换，因为后续组要靠 `shell.command("名字")` 反查前置命令（如 setup 反查 `vpr`、其余组反查 `build_fabric`）来构造依赖边。

引入的关键术语：**命令类别**（仅用于 `help` 分组显示的标签）、**Shell<T>**（自带命令框架，`T` 为 `OpenfpgaContext`）、**MACRO 命令**（自带解析器、shell 透传整行参数的黑盒，如 `vpr`）、**执行函数类型**（`set_command_const_execute_function` 只读 context、`set_command_execute_function` 可写 context）、**命令依赖**（运行时硬约束，非注释）。

核心结论：每条命令遵循**四步注册法**——建 `Command` 加选项 → `add_command` 取 id → `set_command_class` 归类 → 绑定执行函数并可选声明依赖。setup 组主链为 `read_openfpga_arch → link_openfpga_arch → build_fabric`；bitstream 组有教科书级依赖链 `repack → build_architecture_bitstream → build_fabric_bitstream → write_fabric_bitstream`。`shell.tpp` 的 `execute_command` 在调用执行函数前遍历依赖列表，前置命令未运行或失败即报错返回，依赖只检查一级、不递归。**Basic 组必须最后注册**，且组内 `help` 必须最后添加，因其执行函数要对已注册命令集做快照来打印帮助。
### u2-l3 OpenfpgaContext：贯穿全流程的全局数据中枢

本讲讲解 OpenfpgaContext——OpenFPGA shell 中命令间交换数据的唯一全局中枢。它由 OpenfpgaShell 唯一持有，只创建一次、不可拷贝，继承自 VPR 的 Context，并聚合架构（arch_）、Fabric 模块图（module_graph_）、两级比特流（bitstream_manager_/fabric_bitstream_）、多路选择器库（mux_lib_）等核心数据。关键设计是 const/mutable 两套访问器：arch() 只读返回 const T&，mutable_arch() 可写返回 T&，命名即意图，并与 set_command_const_execute_function（只读）/set_command_execute_function（可写）两种执行函数一一对应，使「命令是否改数据」在注册时即声明。FlowManager 记录跨命令的流程级开关（如 compress_routing），由产生副作用的命令写入、供下游查询。核心结论：命令的先后顺序本质就是 Context 各分区的读写依赖；本讲将 arch_ 视为黑盒，留待 u3 打开。

- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. 1	<?xml version="1.0"?>
2	<!-- 
3	  Architecture with no fracturable LUTs
4	
5	  - 40 nm technology
6	  - General purpose logic block: 
7	    K = 4, N = 4
8	  - Routing architecture: L = 4, fc_in = 0.15, Fc_out = 0.1
9	
10	  Details on Modelling:
11	
12	  Based on flagship k6_frac_N10_mem32K_40nm.xml architecture.  This architecture has no fracturable LUTs nor any heterogeneous blocks.
13	
14	
15	  Authors: Jason Luu, Jeff Goeders, Vaughn Betz
16	-->
17	<architecture>
18	  <!-- 
19	       ODIN II specific config begins 
20	       Describes the types of user-specified netlist blocks (in blif, this corresponds to 
21	       ".model [type_of_block]") that this architecture supports.
22	
23	       Note: Basic LUTs, I/Os, and flip-flops are not included here as there are 
24	       already special structures in blif (.names, .input, .output, and .latch) 
25	       that describe them.
26	  -->
27	  <models>
28	    <!-- A virtual model for I/O to be used in the physical mode of io block -->
29	    <model name="io">
30	      <input_ports>
31	        <port name="outpad"/>
32	      </input_ports>
33	      <output_ports>
34	        <port name="inpad"/>
35	      </output_ports>
36	    </model>
37	  </models>
38	  <tiles>
39	    <tile name="io" area="0">
40	      <sub_tile name="io" capacity="8">
41	        <equivalent_sites>
42	          <site pb_type="io"/>
43	        </equivalent_sites>
44	        <input name="outpad" num_pins="1"/>
45	        <output name="inpad" num_pins="1"/>
46	        <fc in_type="frac" in_val="0.15" out_type="frac" out_val="0.10"/>
47	        <pinlocations pattern="custom">
48	          <loc side="left">io.outpad io.inpad</loc>
49	          <loc side="top">io.outpad io.inpad</loc>
50	          <loc side="right">io.outpad io.inpad</loc>
51	          <loc side="bottom">io.outpad io.inpad</loc>
52	        </pinlocations>
53	      </sub_tile>
54	    </tile>
55	    <tile name="clb" area="53894">
56	      <sub_tile name="clb">
57	        <equivalent_sites>
58	          <site pb_type="clb"/>
59	        </equivalent_sites>
60	        <input name="I" num_pins="10" equivalent="full"/>
61	        <output name="O" num_pins="4" equivalent="none"/>
62	        <clock name="clk" num_pins="1"/>
63	        <fc in_type="frac" in_val="0.15" out_type="frac" out_val="0.10"/>
64	        <pinlocations pattern="spread"/>
65	      </sub_tile>
66	    </tile>
67	  </tiles>
68	  <!-- ODIN II specific config ends -->
69	  <!-- Physical descriptions begin -->
70	  <layout tileable="true" concat_wire="false" concat_pass_wire="true">
71	    <auto_layout aspect_ratio="1.0">
72	      <!--Perimeter of 'io' blocks with 'EMPTY' blocks at corners-->
73	      <perimeter type="io" priority="100"/>
74	      <corners type="EMPTY" priority="101"/>
75	      <!--Fill with 'clb'-->
76	      <fill type="clb" priority="10"/>
77	    </auto_layout>
78	    <fixed_layout name="2x2" width="4" height="4">
79	      <!--Perimeter of 'io' blocks with 'EMPTY' blocks at corners-->
80	      <perimeter type="io" priority="100"/>
81	      <corners type="EMPTY" priority="101"/>
82	      <!--Fill with 'clb'-->
83	      <fill type="clb" priority="10"/>
84	    </fixed_layout>
85	    <fixed_layout name="4x4" width="6" height="6">
86	      <!--Perimeter of 'io' blocks with 'EMPTY' blocks at corners-->
87	      <perimeter type="io" priority="100"/>
88	      <corners type="EMPTY" priority="101"/>
89	      <!--Fill with 'clb'-->
90	      <fill type="clb" priority="10"/>
91	    </fixed_layout>
92	    <fixed_layout name="48x48" width="50" height="50">
93	      <!--Perimeter of 'io' blocks with 'EMPTY' blocks at corners-->
94	      <perimeter type="io" priority="100"/>
95	      <corners type="EMPTY" priority="101"/>
96	      <!--Fill with 'clb'-->
97	      <fill type="clb" priority="10"/>
98	    </fixed_layout>
99	    <fixed_layout name="72x72" width="74" height="74">
100	      <!--Perimeter of 'io' blocks with 'EMPTY' blocks at corners-->
101	      <perimeter type="io" priority="100"/>
102	      <corners type="EMPTY" priority="101"/>
103	      <!--Fill with 'clb'-->
104	      <fill type="clb" priority="10"/>
105	    </fixed_layout>
106	    <fixed_layout name="96x96" width="98" height="98">
107	      <!--Perimeter of 'io' blocks with 'EMPTY' blocks at corners-->
108	      <perimeter type="io" priority="100"/>
109	      <corners type="EMPTY" priority="101"/>
110	      <!--Fill with 'clb'-->
111	      <fill type="clb" priority="10"/>
112	    </fixed_layout>
113	  </layout>
114	  <device>
115	    <!-- VB & JL: Using Ian Kuon's transistor sizing and drive strength data for routing, at 40 nm. Ian used BPTM 
116				     models. We are modifying the delay values however, to include metal C and R, which allows more architecture
117				     experimentation. We are also modifying the relative resistance of PMOS to be 1.8x that of NMOS
118				     (vs. Ian's 3x) as 1.8x lines up with Jeff G's data from a 45 nm process (and is more typical of 
119				     45 nm in general). I'm upping the Rmin_nmos from Ian's just over 6k to nearly 9k, and dropping 
120				     RminW_pmos from 18k to 16k to hit this 1.8x ratio, while keeping the delays of buffers approximately
121				     lined up with Stratix IV. 
122				     We are using Jeff G.'s capacitance data for 45 nm (in tech/ptm_45nm).
123				     Jeff's tables list C in for transistors with widths in multiples of the minimum feature size (45 nm).
124				     The minimum contactable transistor is 2.5 * 45 nm, so I need to multiply drive strength sizes in this file
125		                     by 2.5x when looking up in Jeff's tables.
126				     The delay values are lined up with Stratix IV, which has an architecture similar to this
127				     proposed FPGA, and which is also 40 nm 
128				     C_ipin_cblock: input capacitance of a track buffer, which VPR assumes is a single-stage
129				     4x minimum drive strength buffer. -->
130	    <sizing R_minW_nmos="8926" R_minW_pmos="16067"/>
131	    <!-- The grid_logic_tile_area below will be used for all blocks that do not explicitly set their own (non-routing)
132	     	  area; set to 0 since we explicitly set the area of all blocks currently in this architecture file.
133		  -->
134	    <area grid_logic_tile_area="0"/>
135	    <chan_width_distr>
136	      <x distr="uniform" peak="1.000000"/>
137	      <y distr="uniform" peak="1.000000"/>
138	    </chan_width_distr>
139	    <switch_block type="wilton" fs="3"/>
140	    <connection_block input_switch_name="ipin_cblock"/>
141	  </device>
142	  <switchlist>
143	    <!-- VB: the mux_trans_size and buf_size data below is in minimum width transistor *areas*, assuming the purple
144		       book area formula. This means the mux transistors are about 5x minimum drive strength.
145		       We assume the first stage of the buffer is 3x min drive strength to be reasonable given the large 
146		       mux transistors, and this gives a reasonable stage ratio of a bit over 5x to the second stage. We assume
147		       the n and p transistors in the first stage are equal-sized to lower the buffer trip point, since it's fed
148		       by a pass transistor mux. We can then reverse engineer the buffer second stage to hit the specified 
149		       buf_size (really buffer area) - 16.2x minimum drive nmos and 1.8*16.2 = 29.2x minimum drive.
150		       I then took the data from Jeff G.'s PTM modeling of 45 nm to get the Cin (gate of first stage) and Cout 
151		       (diff of second stage) listed below.  Jeff's models are in tech/ptm_45nm, and are in min feature multiples.
152		       The minimum contactable transistor is 2.5 * 45 nm, so I need to multiply the drive strength sizes above by 
153		       2.5x when looking up in Jeff's tables.
154		       Finally, we choose a switch delay (58 ps) that leads to length 4 wires having a delay equal to that of SIV of 126 ps.
155		       This also leads to the switch being 46% of the total wire delay, which is reasonable. -->
156	    <switch type="mux" name="0" R="551" Cin=".77e-15" Cout="4e-15" Tdel="58e-12" mux_trans_size="2.630740" buf_size="27.645901"/>
157	    <!--switch ipin_cblock resistance set to yeild for 4x minimum drive strength buffer-->
158	    <switch type="mux" name="ipin_cblock" R="2231.5" Cout="0." Cin="1.47e-15" Tdel="7.247000e-11" mux_trans_size="1.222260" buf_size="auto"/>
159	  </switchlist>
160	  <segmentlist>
161	    <!--- VB & JL: using ITRS metal stack data, 96 nm half pitch wires, which are intermediate metal width/space.  
162				     With the 96 nm half pitch, such wires would take 60 um of height, vs. a 90 nm high (approximated as square) Stratix IV tile so this seems
163				     reasonable. Using a tile length of 90 nm, corresponding to the length of a Stratix IV tile if it were square. -->
164	    <segment name="L4" freq="1.000000" length="4" type="unidir" Rmetal="101" Cmetal="22.5e-15">
165	      <mux name="0"/>
166	      <sb type="pattern">1 1 1 1 1</sb>
167	      <cb type="pattern">1 1 1 1</cb>
168	    </segment>
169	  </segmentlist>
170	  <complexblocklist>
171	    <!-- Define I/O pads begin -->
172	    <!-- Capacity is a unique property of I/Os, it is the maximum number of I/Os that can be placed at the same (X,Y) location on the FPGA -->
173	    <!-- Not sure of the area of an I/O (varies widely), and it's not relevant to the design of the FPGA core, so we're setting it to 0. -->
174	    <pb_type name="io">
175	      <input name="outpad" num_pins="1"/>
176	      <output name="inpad" num_pins="1"/>
177	      <!-- A mode denotes the physical implementation of an I/O 
178	           This mode will be not packable but is mainly used for fabric verilog generation   
179	        -->
180	      <mode name="physical" disable_packing="true">
181	        <pb_type name="iopad" blif_model=".subckt io" num_pb="1">
182	          <input name="outpad" num_pins="1"/>
183	          <output name="inpad" num_pins="1"/>
184	        </pb_type>
185	        <interconnect>
186	          <direct name="outpad" input="io.outpad" output="iopad.outpad">
187	            <delay_constant max="1.394e-11" in_port="io.outpad" out_port="iopad.outpad"/>
188	          </direct>
189	          <direct name="inpad" input="iopad.inpad" output="io.inpad">
190	            <delay_constant max="4.243e-11" in_port="iopad.inpad" out_port="io.inpad"/>
191	          </direct>
192	        </interconnect>
193	      </mode>
194	      <!-- IOs can operate as either inputs or outputs.
195		     Delays below come from Ian Kuon. They are small, so they should be interpreted as
196		     the delays to and from registers in the I/O (and generally I/Os are registered 
197		     today and that is when you timing analyze them.
198		     -->
199	      <mode name="inpad">
200	        <pb_type name="inpad" blif_model=".input" num_pb="1">
201	          <output name="inpad" num_pins="1"/>
202	        </pb_type>
203	        <interconnect>
204	          <direct name="inpad" input="inpad.inpad" output="io.inpad">
205	            <delay_constant max="4.243e-11" in_port="inpad.inpad" out_port="io.inpad"/>
206	          </direct>
207	        </interconnect>
208	      </mode>
209	      <mode name="outpad">
210	        <pb_type name="outpad" blif_model=".output" num_pb="1">
211	          <input name="outpad" num_pins="1"/>
212	        </pb_type>
213	        <interconnect>
214	          <direct name="outpad" input="io.outpad" output="outpad.outpad">
215	            <delay_constant max="1.394e-11" in_port="io.outpad" out_port="outpad.outpad"/>
216	          </direct>
217	        </interconnect>
218	      </mode>
219	      <!-- Every input pin is driven by 15% of the tracks in a channel, every output pin is driven by 10% of the tracks in a channel -->
220	      <!-- IOs go on the periphery of the FPGA, for consistency, 
221	          make it physically equivalent on all sides so that only one definition of I/Os is needed.
222	          If I do not make a physically equivalent definition, then I need to define 4 different I/Os, one for each side of the FPGA
223	        -->
224	      <!-- Place I/Os on the sides of the FPGA -->
225	      <power method="ignore"/>
226	    </pb_type>
227	    <!-- Define I/O pads ends -->
228	    <!-- Define general purpose logic block (CLB) begin -->
229	    <!--- Area calculation: Total Stratix IV tile area is about 8100 um^2, and a minimum width transistor 
230		   area is 60 L^2 yields a tile area of 84375 MWTAs.
231		   Routing at W=300 is 30481 MWTAs, leaving us with a total of 53000 MWTAs for logic block area 
232		   This means that only 37% of our area is in the general routing, and 63% is inside the logic
233		   block. Note that the crossbar / local interconnect is considered part of the logic block
234		   area in this analysis. That is a lower proportion of of routing area than most academics
235		   assume, but note that the total routing area really includes the crossbar, which would push
236		   routing area up significantly, we estimate into the ~70% range. 
237		   -->
238	    <pb_type name="clb">
239	      <input name="I" num_pins="10" equivalent="full"/>
240	      <output name="O" num_pins="4" equivalent="none"/>
241	      <clock name="clk" num_pins="1"/>
242	      <!-- Describe basic logic element.  
243	             Each basic logic element has a 4-LUT that can be optionally registered
244	        -->
245	      <pb_type name="fle" num_pb="4">
246	        <input name="in" num_pins="4"/>
247	        <output name="out" num_pins="1"/>
248	        <clock name="clk" num_pins="1"/>
249	        <!-- 4-LUT mode definition begin -->
250	        <mode name="n1_lut4">
251	          <!-- Define 4-LUT mode -->
252	          <pb_type name="ble4" num_pb="1">
253	            <input name="in" num_pins="4"/>
254	            <output name="out" num_pins="1"/>
255	            <clock name="clk" num_pins="1"/>
256	            <!-- Define LUT -->
257	            <pb_type name="lut4" blif_model=".names" num_pb="1" class="lut">
258	              <input name="in" num_pins="4" port_class="lut_in"/>
259	              <output name="out" num_pins="1" port_class="lut_out"/>
260	              <!-- LUT timing using delay matrix -->
261	              <delay_matrix type="max" in_port="lut4.in" out_port="lut4.out">
262	                261e-12
263	                261e-12
264	                261e-12
265	                261e-12
266	              </delay_matrix>
267	            </pb_type>
268	            <!-- Define flip-flop -->
269	            <pb_type name="ff" blif_model=".latch" num_pb="1" class="flipflop">
270	              <input name="D" num_pins="1" port_class="D"/>
271	              <output name="Q" num_pins="1" port_class="Q"/>
272	              <clock name="clk" num_pins="1" port_class="clock"/>
273	              <T_setup value="66e-12" port="ff.D" clock="clk"/>
274	              <T_clock_to_Q max="124e-12" port="ff.Q" clock="clk"/>
275	            </pb_type>
276	            <interconnect>
277	              <direct name="direct1" input="ble4.in" output="lut4[0:0].in"/>
278	              <direct name="direct2" input="lut4.out" output="ff.D">
279	                <!-- Advanced user option that tells CAD tool to find LUT+FF pairs in netlist -->
280	                <pack_pattern name="ble4" in_port="lut4.out" out_port="ff.D"/>
281	              </direct>
282	              <direct name="direct3" input="ble4.clk" output="ff.clk"/>
283	              <mux name="mux1" input="ff.Q lut4.out" output="ble4.out">
284	                <!-- LUT to output is faster than FF to output on a Stratix IV -->
285	                <delay_constant max="25e-12" in_port="lut4.out" out_port="ble4.out"/>
286	                <delay_constant max="45e-12" in_port="ff.Q" out_port="ble4.out"/>
287	              </mux>
288	            </interconnect>
289	          </pb_type>
290	          <interconnect>
291	            <direct name="direct1" input="fle.in" output="ble4.in"/>
292	            <direct name="direct2" input="ble4.out" output="fle.out[0:0]"/>
293	            <direct name="direct3" input="fle.clk" output="ble4.clk"/>
294	          </interconnect>
295	        </mode>
296	        <!-- 6-LUT mode definition end -->
297	      </pb_type>
298	      <interconnect>
299	        <!-- We use a full crossbar to get logical equivalence at inputs of CLB 
300			     The delays below come from Stratix IV. the delay through a connection block
301			     input mux + the crossbar in Stratix IV is 167 ps. We already have a 72 ps 
302			     delay on the connection block input mux (modeled by Ian Kuon), so the remaining
303			     delay within the crossbar is 95 ps. 
304			     The delays of cluster feedbacks in Stratix IV is 100 ps, when driven by a LUT.
305			     Since all our outputs LUT outputs go to a BLE output, and have a delay of 
306			     25 ps to do so, we subtract 25 ps from the 100 ps delay of a feedback
307			     to get the part that should be marked on the crossbar.	 -->
308	        <complete name="crossbar" input="clb.I fle[3:0].out" output="fle[3:0].in">
309	          <delay_constant max="95e-12" in_port="clb.I" out_port="fle[3:0].in"/>
310	          <delay_constant max="75e-12" in_port="fle[3:0].out" out_port="fle[3:0].in"/>
311	        </complete>
312	        <complete name="clks" input="clb.clk" output="fle[3:0].clk">
313	        </complete>
314	        <!-- This way of specifying direct connection to clb outputs is important because this architecture uses automatic spreading of opins.  
315	               By grouping to output pins in this fashion, if a logic block is completely filled by 6-LUTs, 
316	               then the outputs those 6-LUTs take get evenly distributed across all four sides of the CLB instead of clumped on two sides (which is what happens with a more
317	               naive specification).
318	          -->
319	        <direct name="clbouts1" input="fle[3:0].out" output="clb.O"/>
320	      </interconnect>
321	      <!-- Every input pin is driven by 15% of the tracks in a channel, every output pin is driven by 10% of the tracks in a channel -->
322	      <!-- Place this general purpose logic block in any unspecified column -->
323	    </pb_type>
324	    <!-- Define general purpose logic block (CLB) ends -->
325	  </complexblocklist>
326	</architecture>
327

file.content:
<?xml version="1.0"?>
<!-- 
  Architecture with no fracturable LUTs

  - 40 nm technology
  - General purpose logic block: 
    K = 4, N = 4
  - Routing architecture: L = 4, fc_in = 0.15, Fc_out = 0.1

  Details on Modelling:

  Based on flagship k6_frac_N10_mem32K_40nm.xml architecture.  This architecture has no fracturable LUTs nor any heterogeneous blocks.


  Authors: Jason Luu, Jeff Goeders, Vaughn Betz
-->
<architecture>
  <!-- 
       ODIN II specific config begins 
       Describes the types of user-specified netlist blocks (in blif, this corresponds to 
       ".model [type_of_block]") that this architecture supports.

       Note: Basic LUTs, I/Os, and flip-flops are not included here as there are 
       already special structures in blif (.names, .input, .output, and .latch) 
       that describe them.
  -->
  <models>
    <!-- A virtual model for I/O to be used in the physical mode of io block -->
    <model name="io">
      <input_ports>
        <port name="outpad"/>
      </input_ports>
      <output_ports>
        <port name="inpad"/>
      </output_ports>
    </model>
  </models>
  <tiles>
    <tile name="io" area="0">
      <sub_tile name="io" capacity="8">
        <equivalent_sites>
          <site pb_type="io"/>
        </equivalent_sites>
        <input name="outpad" num_pins="1"/>
        <output name="inpad" num_pins="1"/>
        <fc in_type="frac" in_val="0.15" out_type="frac" out_val="0.10"/>
        <pinlocations pattern="custom">
          <loc side="left">io.outpad io.inpad</loc>
          <loc side="top">io.outpad io.inpad</loc>
          <loc side="right">io.outpad io.inpad</loc>
          <loc side="bottom">io.outpad io.inpad</loc>
        </pinlocations>
      </sub_tile>
    </tile>
    <tile name="clb" area="53894">
      <sub_tile name="clb">
        <equivalent_sites>
          <site pb_type="clb"/>
        </equivalent_sites>
        <input name="I" num_pins="10" equivalent="full"/>
        <output name="O" num_pins="4" equivalent="none"/>
        <clock name="clk" num_pins="1"/>
        <fc in_type="frac" in_val="0.15" out_type="frac" out_val="0.10"/>
        <pinlocations pattern="spread"/>
      </sub_tile>
    </tile>
  </tiles>
  <!-- ODIN II specific config ends -->
  <!-- Physical descriptions begin -->
  <layout tileable="true" concat_wire="false" concat_pass_wire="true">
    <auto_layout aspect_ratio="1.0">
      <!--Perimeter of 'io' blocks with 'EMPTY' blocks at corners-->
      <perimeter type="io" priority="100"/>
      <corners type="EMPTY" priority="101"/>
      <!--Fill with 'clb'-->
      <fill type="clb" priority="10"/>
    </auto_layout>
    <fixed_layout name="2x2" width="4" height="4">
      <!--Perimeter of 'io' blocks with 'EMPTY' blocks at corners-->
      <perimeter type="io" priority="100"/>
      <corners type="EMPTY" priority="101"/>
      <!--Fill with 'clb'-->
      <fill type="clb" priority="10"/>
    </fixed_layout>
    <fixed_layout name="4x4" width="6" height="6">
      <!--Perimeter of 'io' blocks with 'EMPTY' blocks at corners-->
      <perimeter type="io" priority="100"/>
      <corners type="EMPTY" priority="101"/>
      <!--Fill with 'clb'-->
      <fill type="clb" priority="10"/>
    </fixed_layout>
    <fixed_layout name="48x48" width="50" height="50">
      <!--Perimeter of 'io' blocks with 'EMPTY' blocks at corners-->
      <perimeter type="io" priority="100"/>
      <corners type="EMPTY" priority="101"/>
      <!--Fill with 'clb'-->
      <fill type="clb" priority="10"/>
    </fixed_layout>
    <fixed_layout name="72x72" width="74" height="74">
      <!--Perimeter of 'io' blocks with 'EMPTY' blocks at corners-->
      <perimeter type="io" priority="100"/>
      <corners type="EMPTY" priority="101"/>
      <!--Fill with 'clb'-->
      <fill type="clb" priority="10"/>
    </fixed_layout>
    <fixed_layout name="96x96" width="98" height="98">
      <!--Perimeter of 'io' blocks with 'EMPTY' blocks at corners-->
      <perimeter type="io" priority="100"/>
      <corners type="EMPTY" priority="101"/>
      <!--Fill with 'clb'-->
      <fill type="clb" priority="10"/>
    </fixed_layout>
  </layout>
  <device>
    <!-- VB & JL: Using Ian Kuon's transistor sizing and drive strength data for routing, at 40 nm. Ian used BPTM 
			     models. We are modifying the delay values however, to include metal C and R, which allows more architecture
			     experimentation. We are also modifying the relative resistance of PMOS to be 1.8x that of NMOS
			     (vs. Ian's 3x) as 1.8x lines up with Jeff G's data from a 45 nm process (and is more typical of 
			     45 nm in general). I'm upping the Rmin_nmos from Ian's just over 6k to nearly 9k, and dropping 
			     RminW_pmos from 18k to 16k to hit this 1.8x ratio, while keeping the delays of buffers approximately
			     lined up with Stratix IV. 
			     We are using Jeff G.'s capacitance data for 45 nm (in tech/ptm_45nm).
			     Jeff's tables list C in for transistors with widths in multiples of the minimum feature size (45 nm).
			     The minimum contactable transistor is 2.5 * 45 nm, so I need to multiply drive strength sizes in this file
	                     by 2.5x when looking up in Jeff's tables.
			     The delay values are lined up with Stratix IV, which has an architecture similar to this
			     proposed FPGA, and which is also 40 nm 
			     C_ipin_cblock: input capacitance of a track buffer, which VPR assumes is a single-stage
			     4x minimum drive strength buffer. -->
    <sizing R_minW_nmos="8926" R_minW_pmos="16067"/>
    <!-- The grid_logic_tile_area below will be used for all blocks that do not explicitly set their own (non-routing)
     	  area; set to 0 since we explicitly set the area of all blocks currently in this architecture file.
	  -->
    <area grid_logic_tile_area="0"/>
    <chan_width_distr>
      <x distr="uniform" peak="1.000000"/>
      <y distr="uniform" peak="1.000000"/>
    </chan_width_distr>
    <switch_block type="wilton" fs="3"/>
    <connection_block input_switch_name="ipin_cblock"/>
  </device>
  <switchlist>
    <!-- VB: the mux_trans_size and buf_size data below is in minimum width transistor *areas*, assuming the purple
	       book area formula. This means the mux transistors are about 5x minimum drive strength.
	       We assume the first stage of the buffer is 3x min drive strength to be reasonable given the large 
	       mux transistors, and this gives a reasonable stage ratio of a bit over 5x to the second stage. We assume
	       the n and p transistors in the first stage are equal-sized to lower the buffer trip point, since it's fed
	       by a pass transistor mux. We can then reverse engineer the buffer second stage to hit the specified 
	       buf_size (really buffer area) - 16.2x minimum drive nmos and 1.8*16.2 = 29.2x minimum drive.
	       I then took the data from Jeff G.'s PTM modeling of 45 nm to get the Cin (gate of first stage) and Cout 
	       (diff of second stage) listed below.  Jeff's models are in tech/ptm_45nm, and are in min feature multiples.
	       The minimum contactable transistor is 2.5 * 45 nm, so I need to multiply the drive strength sizes above by 
	       2.5x when looking up in Jeff's tables.
	       Finally, we choose a switch delay (58 ps) that leads to length 4 wires having a delay equal to that of SIV of 126 ps.
	       This also leads to the switch being 46% of the total wire delay, which is reasonable. -->
    <switch type="mux" name="0" R="551" Cin=".77e-15" Cout="4e-15" Tdel="58e-12" mux_trans_size="2.630740" buf_size="27.645901"/>
    <!--switch ipin_cblock resistance set to yeild for 4x minimum drive strength buffer-->
    <switch type="mux" name="ipin_cblock" R="2231.5" Cout="0." Cin="1.47e-15" Tdel="7.247000e-11" mux_trans_size="1.222260" buf_size="auto"/>
  </switchlist>
  <segmentlist>
    <!--- VB & JL: using ITRS metal stack data, 96 nm half pitch wires, which are intermediate metal width/space.  
			     With the 96 nm half pitch, such wires would take 60 um of height, vs. a 90 nm high (approximated as square) Stratix IV tile so this seems
			     reasonable. Using a tile length of 90 nm, corresponding to the length of a Stratix IV tile if it were square. -->
    <segment name="L4" freq="1.000000" length="4" type="unidir" Rmetal="101" Cmetal="22.5e-15">
      <mux name="0"/>
      <sb type="pattern">1 1 1 1 1</sb>
      <cb type="pattern">1 1 1 1</cb>
    </segment>
  </segmentlist>
  <complexblocklist>
    <!-- Define I/O pads begin -->
    <!-- Capacity is a unique property of I/Os, it is the maximum number of I/Os that can be placed at the same (X,Y) location on the FPGA -->
    <!-- Not sure of the area of an I/O (varies widely), and it's not relevant to the design of the FPGA core, so we're setting it to 0. -->
    <pb_type name="io">
      <input name="outpad" num_pins="1"/>
      <output name="inpad" num_pins="1"/>
      <!-- A mode denotes the physical implementation of an I/O 
           This mode will be not packable but is mainly used for fabric verilog generation   
        -->
      <mode name="physical" disable_packing="true">
        <pb_type name="iopad" blif_model=".subckt io" num_pb="1">
          <input name="outpad" num_pins="1"/>
          <output name="inpad" num_pins="1"/>
        </pb_type>
        <interconnect>
          <direct name="outpad" input="io.outpad" output="iopad.outpad">
            <delay_constant max="1.394e-11" in_port="io.outpad" out_port="iopad.outpad"/>
          </direct>
          <direct name="inpad" input="iopad.inpad" output="io.inpad">
            <delay_constant max="4.243e-11" in_port="iopad.inpad" out_port="io.inpad"/>
          </direct>
        </interconnect>
      </mode>
      <!-- IOs can operate as either inputs or outputs.
	     Delays below come from Ian Kuon. They are small, so they should be interpreted as
	     the delays to and from registers in the I/O (and generally I/Os are registered 
	     today and that is when you timing analyze them.
	     -->
      <mode name="inpad">
        <pb_type name="inpad" blif_model=".input" num_pb="1">
          <output name="inpad" num_pins="1"/>
        </pb_type>
        <interconnect>
          <direct name="inpad" input="inpad.inpad" output="io.inpad">
            <delay_constant max="4.243e-11" in_port="inpad.inpad" out_port="io.inpad"/>
          </direct>
        </interconnect>
      </mode>
      <mode name="outpad">
        <pb_type name="outpad" blif_model=".output" num_pb="1">
          <input name="outpad" num_pins="1"/>
        </pb_type>
        <interconnect>
          <direct name="outpad" input="io.outpad" output="outpad.outpad">
            <delay_constant max="1.394e-11" in_port="io.outpad" out_port="outpad.outpad"/>
          </direct>
        </interconnect>
      </mode>
      <!-- Every input pin is driven by 15% of the tracks in a channel, every output pin is driven by 10% of the tracks in a channel -->
      <!-- IOs go on the periphery of the FPGA, for consistency, 
          make it physically equivalent on all sides so that only one definition of I/Os is needed.
          If I do not make a physically equivalent definition, then I need to define 4 different I/Os, one for each side of the FPGA
        -->
      <!-- Place I/Os on the sides of the FPGA -->
      <power method="ignore"/>
    </pb_type>
    <!-- Define I/O pads ends -->
    <!-- Define general purpose logic block (CLB) begin -->
    <!--- Area calculation: Total Stratix IV tile area is about 8100 um^2, and a minimum width transistor 
	   area is 60 L^2 yields a tile area of 84375 MWTAs.
	   Routing at W=300 is 30481 MWTAs, leaving us with a total of 53000 MWTAs for logic block area 
	   This means that only 37% of our area is in the general routing, and 63% is inside the logic
	   block. Note that the crossbar / local interconnect is considered part of the logic block
	   area in this analysis. That is a lower proportion of of routing area than most academics
	   assume, but note that the total routing area really includes the crossbar, which would push
	   routing area up significantly, we estimate into the ~70% range. 
	   -->
    <pb_type name="clb">
      <input name="I" num_pins="10" equivalent="full"/>
      <output name="O" num_pins="4" equivalent="none"/>
      <clock name="clk" num_pins="1"/>
      <!-- Describe basic logic element.  
             Each basic logic element has a 4-LUT that can be optionally registered
        -->
      <pb_type name="fle" num_pb="4">
        <input name="in" num_pins="4"/>
        <output name="out" num_pins="1"/>
        <clock name="clk" num_pins="1"/>
        <!-- 4-LUT mode definition begin -->
        <mode name="n1_lut4">
          <!-- Define 4-LUT mode -->
          <pb_type name="ble4" num_pb="1">
            <input name="in" num_pins="4"/>
            <output name="out" num_pins="1"/>
            <clock name="clk" num_pins="1"/>
            <!-- Define LUT -->
            <pb_type name="lut4" blif_model=".names" num_pb="1" class="lut">
              <input name="in" num_pins="4" port_class="lut_in"/>
              <output name="out" num_pins="1" port_class="lut_out"/>
              <!-- LUT timing using delay matrix -->
              <delay_matrix type="max" in_port="lut4.in" out_port="lut4.out">
                261e-12
                261e-12
                261e-12
                261e-12
              </delay_matrix>
            </pb_type>
            <!-- Define flip-flop -->
            <pb_type name="ff" blif_model=".latch" num_pb="1" class="flipflop">
              <input name="D" num_pins="1" port_class="D"/>
              <output name="Q" num_pins="1" port_class="Q"/>
              <clock name="clk" num_pins="1" port_class="clock"/>
              <T_setup value="66e-12" port="ff.D" clock="clk"/>
              <T_clock_to_Q max="124e-12" port="ff.Q" clock="clk"/>
            </pb_type>
            <interconnect>
              <direct name="direct1" input="ble4.in" output="lut4[0:0].in"/>
              <direct name="direct2" input="lut4.out" output="ff.D">
                <!-- Advanced user option that tells CAD tool to find LUT+FF pairs in netlist -->
                <pack_pattern name="ble4" in_port="lut4.out" out_port="ff.D"/>
              </direct>
              <direct name="direct3" input="ble4.clk" output="ff.clk"/>
              <mux name="mux1" input="ff.Q lut4.out" output="ble4.out">
                <!-- LUT to output is faster than FF to output on a Stratix IV -->
                <delay_constant max="25e-12" in_port="lut4.out" out_port="ble4.out"/>
                <delay_constant max="45e-12" in_port="ff.Q" out_port="ble4.out"/>
              </mux>
            </interconnect>
          </pb_type>
          <interconnect>
            <direct name="direct1" input="fle.in" output="ble4.in"/>
            <direct name="direct2" input="ble4.out" output="fle.out[0:0]"/>
            <direct name="direct3" input="fle.clk" output="ble4.clk"/>
          </interconnect>
        </mode>
        <!-- 6-LUT mode definition end -->
      </pb_type>
      <interconnect>
        <!-- We use a full crossbar to get logical equivalence at inputs of CLB 
		     The delays below come from Stratix IV. the delay through a connection block
		     input mux + the crossbar in Stratix IV is 167 ps. We already have a 72 ps 
		     delay on the connection block input mux (modeled by Ian Kuon), so the remaining
		     delay within the crossbar is 95 ps. 
		     The delays of cluster feedbacks in Stratix IV is 100 ps, when driven by a LUT.
		     Since all our outputs LUT outputs go to a BLE output, and have a delay of 
		     25 ps to do so, we subtract 25 ps from the 100 ps delay of a feedback
		     to get the part that should be marked on the crossbar.	 -->
        <complete name="crossbar" input="clb.I fle[3:0].out" output="fle[3:0].in">
          <delay_constant max="95e-12" in_port="clb.I" out_port="fle[3:0].in"/>
          <delay_constant max="75e-12" in_port="fle[3:0].out" out_port="fle[3:0].in"/>
        </complete>
        <complete name="clks" input="clb.clk" output="fle[3:0].clk">
        </complete>
        <!-- This way of specifying direct connection to clb outputs is important because this architecture uses automatic spreading of opins.  
               By grouping to output pins in this fashion, if a logic block is completely filled by 6-LUTs, 
               then the outputs those 6-LUTs take get evenly distributed across all four sides of the CLB instead of clumped on two sides (which is what happens with a more
               naive specification).
          -->
        <direct name="clbouts1" input="fle[3:0].out" output="clb.O"/>
      </interconnect>
      <!-- Every input pin is driven by 15% of the tracks in a channel, every output pin is driven by 10% of the tracks in a channel -->
      <!-- Place this general purpose logic block in any unspecified column -->
    </pb_type>
    <!-- Define general purpose logic block (CLB) ends -->
  </complexblocklist>
</architecture>

1	# = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = =
2	# Configuration file for running experiments
3	# = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = =
4	# timeout_each_job : FPGA Task script splits fpga flow into multiple jobs
5	# Each job execute fpga_flow script on combination of architecture & benchmark
6	# timeout_each_job is timeout for each job
7	# = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = =
8	
9	[GENERAL]
10	run_engine=openfpga_shell
11	power_tech_file = ${PATH:OPENFPGA_PATH}/openfpga_flow/tech/PTM_45nm/45nm.xml
12	power_analysis = true
13	spice_output=false
14	verilog_output=true
15	timeout_each_job = 20*60
16	fpga_flow=yosys_vpr
17	
18	[OpenFPGA_SHELL]
19	openfpga_shell_template=${PATH:OPENFPGA_PATH}/openfpga_flow/openfpga_shell_scripts/write_full_testbench_example_script.openfpga
20	openfpga_arch_file=${PATH:OPENFPGA_PATH}/openfpga_flow/openfpga_arch/k4_N4_40nm_cc_openfpga.xml
21	openfpga_sim_setting_file=${PATH:OPENFPGA_PATH}/openfpga_flow/openfpga_simulation_settings/auto_sim_openfpga.xml
22	openfpga_vpr_device_layout=
23	openfpga_fast_configuration=
24	
25	[ARCHITECTURES]
26	arch0=${PATH:OPENFPGA_PATH}/openfpga_flow/vpr_arch/k4_N4_tileable_40nm.xml
27	
28	[BENCHMARKS]
29	bench0=${PATH:OPENFPGA_PATH}/openfpga_flow/benchmarks/micro_benchmark/and2/and2.v
30	bench1=${PATH:OPENFPGA_PATH}/openfpga_flow/benchmarks/micro_benchmark/or2/or2.v
31	bench2=${PATH:OPENFPGA_PATH}/openfpga_flow/benchmarks/micro_benchmark/and2_latch/and2_latch.v
32	
33	[SYNTHESIS_PARAM]

35	bench0_top = and2
36	bench0_chan_width = 300
37	
38	bench1_top = or2
39	bench1_chan_width = 300
40	
41	bench2_top = and2_latch
42	bench2_chan_width = 300

44	[SCRIPT_PARAM_MIN_ROUTE_CHAN_WIDTH]
45	end_flow_with_test=

file.content:
# = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = =
# Configuration file for running experiments
# = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = =
# timeout_each_job : FPGA Task script splits fpga flow into multiple jobs
# Each job execute fpga_flow script on combination of architecture & benchmark
# timeout_each_job is timeout for each job
# = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = =

[GENERAL]
run_engine=openfpga_shell
power_tech_file = ${PATH:OPENFPGA_PATH}/openfpga_flow/tech/PTM_45nm/45nm.xml
power_analysis = true
spice_output=false
verilog_output=true
timeout_each_job = 20*60
fpga_flow=yosys_vpr

[OpenFPGA_SHELL]
openfpga_shell_template=${PATH:OPENFPGA_PATH}/openfpga_flow/openfpga_shell_scripts/write_full_testbench_example_script.openfpga
openfpga_arch_file=${PATH:OPENFPGA_PATH}/openfpga_flow/openfpga_arch/k4_N4_40nm_cc_openfpga.xml
openfpga_sim_setting_file=${PATH:OPENFPGA_PATH}/openfpga_flow/openfpga_simulation_settings/auto_sim_openfpga.xml
openfpga_vpr_device_layout=
openfpga_fast_configuration=

[ARCHITECTURES]
arch0=${PATH:OPENFPGA_PATH}/openfpga_flow/vpr_arch/k4_N4_tileable_40nm.xml

[BENCHMARKS]
bench0=${PATH:OPENFPGA_PATH}/openfpga_flow/benchmarks/micro_benchmark/and2/and2.v
bench1=${PATH:OPENFPGA_PATH}/openfpga_flow/benchmarks/micro_benchmark/or2/or2.v
bench2=${PATH:OPENFPGA_PATH}/openfpga_flow/benchmarks/micro_benchmark/and2_latch/and2_latch.v

[SYNTHESIS_PARAM]
bench_read_verilog_options_common = -nolatches
bench0_top = and2
bench0_chan_width = 300

bench1_top = or2
bench1_chan_width = 300

bench2_top = and2_latch
bench2_chan_width = 300

[SCRIPT_PARAM_MIN_ROUTE_CHAN_WIDTH]
end_flow_with_test=

aiTitle: OpenFPGA 两套架构文件讲解

content.thinking: Now let me read the openfpga_arch.xml file. Let me also check if there are other arch files for comparison (like a bank version) to better explain the naming convention. Let me also verify the existence of the directories mentioned.

content.text: 现在让我读取 `openfpga_arch.xml` 文件，并浏览该目录以收集命名规范的示例。

content.content:
1	<?xml version="1.0"?>
2	<!-- Architecture annotation for OpenFPGA framework
3	     This annotation supports the k6_N10_40nm.xml 
4	     - General purpose logic block
5	       - K = 6, N = 10, I = 40
6	       - Single mode
7	     - Routing architecture
8	       - L = 4, fc_in = 0.15, fc_out = 0.1
9	  -->
10	<openfpga_architecture>
11	  <technology_library>
12	    <device_library>
13	      <device_model name="logic" type="transistor">
14	        <lib type="industry" corner="TOP_TT" ref="M" path="${OPENFPGA_PATH}/openfpga_flow/tech/PTM_45nm/45nm.pm"/>
15	        <design vdd="0.9" pn_ratio="2"/>
16	        <pmos name="pch" chan_length="40e-9" min_width="140e-9" variation="logic_transistor_var"/>
17	        <nmos name="nch" chan_length="40e-9" min_width="140e-9" variation="logic_transistor_var"/>

153	      <design_technology type="cmos"/>

<?xml version="1.0"?>
<!-- Architecture annotation for OpenFPGA framework
     This annotation supports the k6_N10_40nm.xml 
     - General purpose logic block
       - K = 6, N = 10, I = 40
       - Single mode
     - Routing architecture
       - L = 4, fc_in = 0.15, fc_out = 0.1
  -->
<openfpga_architecture>
  <technology_library>
    <device_library>
      <device_model name="logic" type="transistor">
        <lib type="industry" corner="TOP_TT" ref="M" path="${OPENFPGA_PATH}/openfpga_flow/tech/PTM_45nm/45nm.pm"/>
        <design vdd="0.9" pn_ratio="2"/>
        <pmos name="pch" chan_length="40e-9" min_width="140e-9" variation="logic_transistor_var"/>
        <nmos name="nch" chan_length="40e-9" min_width="140e-9" variation="logic_transistor_var"/>
      </device_model>
      <device_model name="io" type="transistor">
        <lib type="academia" ref="M" path="${OPENFPGA_PATH}/openfpga_flow/tech/PTM_45nm/45nm.pm"/>
        <design vdd="2.5" pn_ratio="3"/>
        <pmos name="pch_25" chan_length="270e-9" min_width="320e-9" variation="io_transistor_var"/>
        <nmos name="nch_25" chan_length="270e-9" min_width="320e-9" variation="io_transistor_var"/>
      </device_model>
    </device_library>
    <variation_library>
      <variation name="logic_transistor_var" abs_deviation="0.1" num_sigma="3"/>
      <variation name="io_transistor_var" abs_deviation="0.1" num_sigma="3"/>
    </variation_library>
  </technology_library>
  <circuit_library>
    <circuit_model type="inv_buf" name="INVTX1" prefix="INVTX1" is_default="true">
      <design_technology type="cmos" topology="inverter" size="1"/>
      <device_technology device_model_name="logic"/>
      <port type="input" prefix="in" size="1"/>
      <port type="output" prefix="out" size="1"/>
      <delay_matrix type="rise" in_port="in" out_port="out">
        10e-12
      </delay_matrix>
      <delay_matrix type="fall" in_port="in" out_port="out">
        10e-12
      </delay_matrix>
    </circuit_model>
    <circuit_model type="inv_buf" name="buf4" prefix="buf4" is_default="false">
      <design_technology type="cmos" topology="buffer" size="1" num_level="2" f_per_stage="4"/>
      <device_technology device_model_name="logic"/>
      <port type="input" prefix="in" size="1"/>
      <port type="output" prefix="out" size="1"/>
      <delay_matrix type="rise" in_port="in" out_port="out">
        10e-12
      </delay_matrix>
      <delay_matrix type="fall" in_port="in" out_port="out">
        10e-12
      </delay_matrix>
    </circuit_model>
    <circuit_model type="inv_buf" name="tap_buf4" prefix="tap_buf4" is_default="false">
      <design_technology type="cmos" topology="buffer" size="1" num_level="3" f_per_stage="4"/>
      <device_technology device_model_name="logic"/>
      <port type="input" prefix="in" size="1"/>
      <port type="output" prefix="out" size="1"/>
      <delay_matrix type="rise" in_port="in" out_port="out">
        10e-12
      </delay_matrix>
      <delay_matrix type="fall" in_port="in" out_port="out">
        10e-12
      </delay_matrix>
    </circuit_model>
    <circuit_model type="pass_gate" name="TGATE" prefix="TGATE" is_default="true">
      <design_technology type="cmos" topology="transmission_gate" nmos_size="1" pmos_size="2"/>
      <device_technology device_model_name="logic"/>
      <input_buffer exist="false"/>
      <output_buffer exist="false"/>
      <port type="input" prefix="in" size="1"/>
      <port type="input" prefix="sel" size="1"/>
      <port type="input" prefix="selb" size="1"/>
      <port type="output" prefix="out" size="1"/>
      <delay_matrix type="rise" in_port="in sel selb" out_port="out">
        10e-12 5e-12 5e-12
      </delay_matrix>
      <delay_matrix type="fall" in_port="in sel selb" out_port="out">
        10e-12 5e-12 5e-12
      </delay_matrix>
    </circuit_model>
    <circuit_model type="chan_wire" name="chan_segment" prefix="track_seg" is_default="true">
      <design_technology type="cmos"/>
      <input_buffer exist="false"/>
      <output_buffer exist="false"/>
      <port type="input" prefix="in" size="1"/>
      <port type="output" prefix="out" size="1"/>
      <wire_param model_type="pi" R="101" C="22.5e-15" num_level="1"/>
      <!-- model_type could be T, res_val and cap_val DON'T CARE -->
    </circuit_model>
    <circuit_model type="wire" name="direct_interc" prefix="direct_interc" is_default="true">
      <design_technology type="cmos"/>
      <input_buffer exist="false"/>
      <output_buffer exist="false"/>
      <port type="input" prefix="in" size="1"/>
      <port type="output" prefix="out" size="1"/>
      <wire_param model_type="pi" R="0" C="0" num_level="1"/>
      <!-- model_type could be T, res_val cap_val should be defined -->
    </circuit_model>
    <circuit_model type="mux" name="mux_tree" prefix="mux_tree" dump_structural_verilog="true">
      <design_technology type="cmos" structure="tree" add_const_input="true" const_input_val="1"/>
      <input_buffer exist="true" circuit_model_name="INVTX1"/>
      <output_buffer exist="true" circuit_model_name="INVTX1"/>
      <pass_gate_logic circuit_model_name="TGATE"/>
      <port type="input" prefix="in" size="1"/>
      <port type="output" prefix="out" size="1"/>
      <port type="sram" prefix="sram" size="1"/>
    </circuit_model>
    <circuit_model type="mux" name="mux_tree_tapbuf" prefix="mux_tree_tapbuf" is_default="true" dump_structural_verilog="true">
      <design_technology type="cmos" structure="tree" add_const_input="true" const_input_val="1"/>
      <input_buffer exist="true" circuit_model_name="INVTX1"/>
      <output_buffer exist="true" circuit_model_name="tap_buf4"/>
      <pass_gate_logic circuit_model_name="TGATE"/>
      <port type="input" prefix="in" size="1"/>
      <port type="output" prefix="out" size="1"/>
      <port type="sram" prefix="sram" size="1"/>
    </circuit_model>
    <!--DFF subckt ports should be defined as <D> <Q> <CLK> <RESET> <SET>  -->
    <circuit_model type="ff" name="DFFSRQ" prefix="DFFSRQ" spice_netlist="${OPENFPGA_PATH}/openfpga_flow/openfpga_cell_library/spice/dff.sp" verilog_netlist="${OPENFPGA_PATH}/openfpga_flow/openfpga_cell_library/verilog/dff.v">
      <design_technology type="cmos"/>
      <input_buffer exist="true" circuit_model_name="INVTX1"/>
      <output_buffer exist="true" circuit_model_name="INVTX1"/>
      <port type="input" prefix="D" size="1"/>
      <port type="input" prefix="set" lib_name="SET" size="1" is_global="true" default_val="0" is_set="true"/>
      <port type="input" prefix="reset" lib_name="RST" size="1" is_global="true" default_val="0" is_reset="true"/>
      <port type="output" prefix="Q" size="1"/>
      <port type="clock" prefix="clk" lib_name="CK" size="1" is_global="true" default_val="0"/>
    </circuit_model>
    <circuit_model type="lut" name="lut4" prefix="lut4" dump_structural_verilog="true">
      <design_technology type="cmos"/>
      <input_buffer exist="true" circuit_model_name="INVTX1"/>
      <output_buffer exist="true" circuit_model_name="INVTX1"/>
      <lut_input_inverter exist="true" circuit_model_name="INVTX1"/>
      <lut_input_buffer exist="true" circuit_model_name="buf4"/>
      <pass_gate_logic circuit_model_name="TGATE"/>
      <port type="input" prefix="in" size="4"/>
      <port type="output" prefix="out" size="1"/>
      <port type="sram" prefix="sram" size="16"/>
    </circuit_model>
    <!--Scan-chain DFF subckt ports should be defined as <D> <Q> <Qb> <CLK> <RESET> <SET>  -->
    <circuit_model type="ccff" name="DFF" prefix="DFF" spice_netlist="${OPENFPGA_PATH}/openfpga_flow/openfpga_cell_library/spice/dff.sp" verilog_netlist="${OPENFPGA_PATH}/openfpga_flow/openfpga_cell_library/verilog/dff.v">
      <design_technology type="cmos"/>
      <input_buffer exist="true" circuit_model_name="INVTX1"/>
      <output_buffer exist="true" circuit_model_name="INVTX1"/>
      <port type="input" prefix="D" size="1"/>
      <port type="output" prefix="Q" size="1"/>
      <port type="output" prefix="QN" size="1"/>
      <port type="clock" prefix="prog_clk" lib_name="CK" size="1" is_global="true" default_val="0" is_prog="true"/>
    </circuit_model>
    <circuit_model type="iopad" name="GPIO" prefix="GPIO" spice_netlist="${OPENFPGA_PATH}/openfpga_flow/openfpga_cell_library/spice/gpio.sp" verilog_netlist="${OPENFPGA_PATH}/openfpga_flow/openfpga_cell_library/verilog/gpio.v">
      <design_technology type="cmos"/>
      <input_buffer exist="true" circuit_model_name="INVTX1"/>
      <output_buffer exist="true" circuit_model_name="INVTX1"/>
      <port type="inout" prefix="PAD" size="1" is_global="true" is_io="true" is_data_io="true"/>
      <port type="sram" prefix="DIR" size="1" mode_select="true" circuit_model_name="DFF" default_val="1"/>
      <port type="input" prefix="outpad" lib_name="A" size="1"/>
      <port type="output" prefix="inpad" lib_name="Y" size="1"/>
    </circuit_model>
  </circuit_library>
  <configuration_protocol>
    <organization type="scan_chain" circuit_model_name="DFF"/>
  </configuration_protocol>
  <connection_block>
    <switch name="ipin_cblock" circuit_model_name="mux_tree_tapbuf"/>
  </connection_block>
  <switch_block>
    <switch name="0" circuit_model_name="mux_tree_tapbuf"/>
  </switch_block>
  <routing_segment>
    <segment name="L4" circuit_model_name="chan_segment"/>
  </routing_segment>
  <pb_type_annotations>
    <!-- physical pb_type binding in complex block IO -->
    <pb_type name="io" physical_mode_name="physical" idle_mode_name="inpad"/>
    <pb_type name="io[physical].iopad" circuit_model_name="GPIO" mode_bits="1"/>
    <pb_type name="io[inpad].inpad" physical_pb_type_name="io[physical].iopad" mode_bits="1"/>
    <pb_type name="io[outpad].outpad" physical_pb_type_name="io[physical].iopad" mode_bits="0"/>
    <!-- End physical pb_type binding in complex block IO -->
    <!-- physical pb_type binding in complex block CLB -->
    <!-- physical mode will be the default mode if not specified -->
    <pb_type name="clb">
      <!-- Binding interconnect to circuit models as their physical implementation, if not defined, we use the default model -->
      <interconnect name="crossbar" circuit_model_name="mux_tree"/>
    </pb_type>
    <pb_type name="clb.fle[n1_lut4].ble4.lut4" circuit_model_name="lut4"/>
    <pb_type name="clb.fle[n1_lut4].ble4.ff" circuit_model_name="DFFSRQ"/>
    <!-- End physical pb_type binding in complex block IO -->
  </pb_type_annotations>
</openfpga_architecture>
